name: Baseline
description: Current state as of Q4 2025. No new regulatory pressure. Companies experiment with 1-5 AI agents. Governance is ad hoc or nonexistent. This is the control scenario against which all others are measured.

name: Regulatory Trigger
description: EU AI Act enforcement expands to cover autonomous agent systems. US follows with NIST standards mandate. Companies face compliance deadlines for AI agent governance. Governance shifts from optional to mandatory.

name: Agent Explosion
description: Agent tooling matures rapidly. Companies deploy 20-100 specialized agents across engineering, operations, and business functions. Complexity makes ad hoc governance untenable. The 'Kubernetes moment' for agents.

name: Budget Crunch
description: AI spending tightens after initial hype cycle. CFOs demand ROI proof. Governance is seen as overhead, not enablement. Companies cut AI infrastructure budgets by 20-30%.

name: Competitor Capture
description: Google bundles basic agent governance into Vertex AI. Microsoft adds fleet monitoring to Azure AI. Basic governance becomes a platform feature. The question becomes: is Admiral's depth needed beyond what platforms provide free?

name: Incident Catalyst
description: A public AI agent failure causes significant financial loss or data breach at a Fortune 500 company. Board-level attention on AI governance. Companies scramble to implement oversight. Insurance companies begin requiring governance certification.

name: Walk-Away
description: Company has budget, need, and capability — but chooses to build governance internally. Tests the thesis that governance is too complex to build in-house. This scenario applies the maximum walk-away penalties to identify which companies would never buy.
